critic_questions_addressed.CQ1: APPLIED — H2 fixed: CaMKII→EP300 replaced with CaMKII→HDAC4/5 nuclear export
critic_questions_addressed.CQ2: ADDRESSED — EH1 frames H3K27me3 at mechanoenhancers as first falsifiable test, not assumption
critic_questions_addressed.CQ3: ADDRESSED — EH3 restricts switch to 5-15 kPa window (fibroblast-relevant), adds fibroblast cell-type specificity note
critic_questions_addressed.CQ4: APPLIED — EH5 replaces verteporfin with IDR-deleted YAP F95A/W96A (Boija 2018)
critic_questions_addressed.CQ5: INTEGRATED — All evolved hypotheses specify CUT&RUN for H3K27me3+H3K27ac+H3K4me1 as prerequisite experiment

evolved_hypotheses.evolved_title: BRD4 IDR Multivalency (n≈4) Creates a 5-15 kPa Mechanical Threshold Switch via Cooperative Condensate Assembly at Mechanoenhancers
evolved_hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: BRD4 IDR multivalent LLPS cooperativity (6 FG-repeat modules, Hill n≈4) → mechanical threshold switch at 5-15 kPa stiffness window
improvements_over_parent: Names molecular basis for Hill n≈4: BRD4 6 FG-repeat IDR modules + YAP WW domain = valency 8, n ≈ valency/2 (Banani 2016 LLPS multivalency model)
Restricts switch to 5-15 kPa window where YAP is still rising — above 20 kPa YAP saturates (PNAS 2021 model), eliminating the plateau wound
Corrects citation: Cai et al. 2019 Nat Cell Biol (not Zanconato 2018) for YAP LLPS condensate evidence; Zanconato 2018 Nat Medicine correctly attributed for YAP-BRD4 co-occupancy
Specific threshold prediction: >3-fold BRD4 condensate volume jump between 5→10 kPa, <1.5-fold between 10→50 kPa (dSTORM at CYR61 locus)
Adds BRD4-ΔCTD deletion mutant as mechanistic test (IDR multivalency → cooperativity → switch; without IDR, switch becomes linear)
wounds_addressed: YAP plateau above 20 kPa: addressed by restricting hypothesis to 5-15 kPa pre-saturation window
Condensates in fibroblast context unverified: addressed by specifying primary lung fibroblasts (same cell type as Cosgrove 2025)
Hill n=2-4 speculative: addressed by grounding in BRD4 IDR multivalency theory (Banani 2016)
key_references: Banani et al. 2016 Science (multivalency → cooperativity, Hill n ≈ valency/2)
Sabari et al. 2018 Science (BRD4-MED1 condensates at super-enhancers)
Cai et al. 2019 Nat Cell Biol (YAP phase separation condensates — corrected citation)
Zanconato et al. 2018 Nat Medicine (YAP-BRD4 co-occupancy at super-enhancers)
Shin et al. 2018 Cell (LLPS cooperativity threshold behavior)
PNAS 2021 YAP spatial model (YAP saturation above 20 kPa)

evolved_hypotheses.evolved_title: G-Actin Threshold-Driven RPEL Conformational Unmasking Enables MRTF-A to Recruit EP300 at CaRG-Box Mechanoenhancers Independent of SRF
evolved_hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: G-actin depletion below 5 μM (stiff ECM) → MRTF-A RPEL conformational opening → cryptic LXXLL motif exposed → SRF-independent EP300 recruitment at CaRG-box mechanoenhancers
improvements_over_parent: Replaces vague 'enhancer preference' with specific molecular mechanism: G-actin threshold → RPEL conformational switch → LXXLL-EP300 interaction
Provides quantitative G-actin thresholds: RPEL Kd ~1 μM per module, cytoplasmic G-actin ~12 μM (soft ECM) → ~3 μM (stiff ECM), switch at ~5 μM
Names specific LXXLL motif: Leu184-Met185-Val186-Leu187-Leu188 (predicted from p160 coactivator homology)
Adds ΔRPEL mutant prediction: constitutively 'open' MRTF-A should show stiffness-independent dual promoter+enhancer binding
Adds LXXLL mutant: L184A/L187A/L188A disrupts EP300 interaction at enhancers without affecting SRF-promoter binding
Promoter bias wound converted into prediction: promoter-dominant binding is the DEFAULT (high G-actin closed state)
wounds_addressed: No mechanism for enhancer redistribution: addressed by G-actin threshold-driven RPEL conformational switch
Promoter-dominant ChIP-seq: converted from weakness into prediction (closed RPEL state → promoter DEFAULT)
key_references: Vartiainen et al. 2007 Science (MRTF-A nuclear actin sensing)
Mouilleron et al. 2008 Nat Struct Mol Biol (RPEL-actin crystal structure, Kd ~1 μM)
Miralles et al. 2003 Cell (MRTF-A nuclear translocation)
Glass & Rosenfeld 2000 Genes Dev (LXXLL-EP300 NR-ID interaction structural basis)
Cosgrove et al. 2025 Science (CaRG motif enrichment at mechanoenhancers)

scores_expected_change.Mechanistic_Specificity: +2 (RPEL Kd values, LXXLL motif, G-actin thresholds all named)
scores_expected_change.Testability: +1 (ΔRPEL + LXXLL mutants are clean mechanistic tests)
scores_expected_change.Groundedness: +0.5 (RPEL Kd values grounded in crystal structure data)
scores_expected_change.Novelty: +0.5 (G-actin threshold → LXXLL unmasking is a novel conformational model)

evolved_hypotheses.evolved_title: Piezo1→Ca2+→CaMKII→HDAC4/5 Nuclear Export Rapidly Derepresses Mechanoenhancers (<15 min) Before YAP Condensate Amplification (30-120 min)
evolved_hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Piezo1→Ca2+→CaMKII→HDAC4-Ser467/HDAC5-Ser498 phosphorylation→14-3-3 binding→nuclear export→EP300 derepression at mechanoenhancers (Phase 1); YAP-BRD4 condensate amplification (Phase 2)

fatal_error_corrected.error: CaMKII does NOT phosphorylate/activate EP300 — this is CaMKIV (Thompson lab Chemical Reviews 2014). CaMKII actually phosphorylates CREB at inhibitory Ser-142.
fatal_error_corrected.fix: CaMKII phosphorylates HDAC4 (Ser467) and HDAC5 (Ser498), causing 14-3-3 binding and nuclear export (Backs 2006 PNAS). HDAC4/5 nuclear export = EP300 DEREPRESSION, not EP300 activation.
fatal_error_corrected.mechanism_type_change: From direct EP300 activation (wrong) to HDAC4/5 derepression of pre-existing EP300 (correct)

improvements_over_parent: Fatal mechanism error corrected: CaMKII→HDAC4/5 nuclear export is experimentally documented (Backs 2006 PNAS, cardiac cells)
Quantitative grounding: HDAC4-GFP nuclear exit t1/2 = ~8 min (Backs 2006 Fig 2); Piezo1 Ca2+ ~400 nM → partial CaMKII activation → ~30-40% HDAC4 export
Two-phase model preserved but mechanistically corrected: Phase 1 = HDAC4/5 derepression (not EP300 activation); Phase 2 = YAP condensate amplification
Phase 1 H3K27ac quantified: ~30-40% of maximum (partial derepression), not full activation
CaMKIV negative control added (STO-609 has no effect) to rule out the confused kinase
HDAC4/5 double knockdown prediction: eliminates Phase 1/2 distinction → monotonic H3K27ac rise
wounds_addressed: Fatal mechanism error (CaMKII≠EP300 kinase): corrected with HDAC4/5 route
Overinterpreted STRING score: STRING EP300-CAMK2A 0.908 reflects pathway co-occurrence; the actual CaMKII substrate at chromatin is HDAC4/5, not EP300
key_references: Backs et al. 2006 PNAS (CaMKII→HDAC4 Ser467 phosphorylation→nuclear export; t1/2 ~8 min measured directly)
McKinsey et al. 2000 Mol Cell Biol (CaMKII→HDAC5 Ser498/632 phosphorylation)
Mihaylova & Shaw 2013 Nat Cell Biol (class IIa HDACs as chromatin repressors antagonizing EP300)
Thompson lab Chemical Reviews 2014 (CaMKIV, not CaMKII, phosphorylates p300/CBP)
Science Advances 2025 (Piezo1→Rho-ROCK→H3K acetylation — parallel pathway validation)

scores_expected_change.Mechanistic_Specificity: +2 (HDAC4 Ser467, HDAC5 Ser498, 14-3-3 binding, t1/2 quantified)
scores_expected_change.Groundedness: +2 (fatal error corrected; mechanism now has direct experimental support from Backs 2006)
scores_expected_change.Testability: +1 (CaMKIV negative control, HDAC4/5 knockdown control added)
scores_expected_change.Impact: -0.5 (HDAC derepression is less surprising than direct CaMKII→EP300 activation)

evolved_hypotheses.evolved_title: ECM Stress Relaxation Rate (tau_1/2) Determines KDM6B Nuclear Retention Duration and H3K27me3 Demethylation Reach: Promoters First, Mechanoenhancers Only with Sustained Nuclear KDM6B
evolved_hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: ECM stress relaxation rate (τ₁/₂) → duration of FAK-Src-PKA signaling → KDM6B nuclear retention time → whether KDM6B demethylation reaches mechanoenhancers (slow-relaxing ECM) or stays at promoters (fast-relaxing ECM)
improvements_over_parent: Adds viscoelasticity dimension (τ₁/₂) as the controlling variable — absent from H1 entirely, adds a new axis grounded in Zhang 2025
Promoter bias wound converted into temporal prediction: promoters are demethylated FIRST (fast kinetics), mechanoenhancers only with sustained KDM6B (slow-relaxing ECM required)
H3K27me3 premise uncertainty made explicit first falsifiable test: if H3K27me3 absent at mechanoenhancers on soft ECM → hypothesis falsified immediately
Provides specific ECM comparison: matched-modulus elastic (50 kPa polyacrylamide) vs viscoelastic (50 kPa alginate) — same elastic modulus, different τ₁/₂
FAK-Src-PKA→KDM6B Ser1179 mechanism named for chromatin retention control
Temporal sequence prediction: promoter H3K27me3 loss 30-60 min before mechanoenhancer H3K27me3 loss
wounds_addressed: KDM6B promoter bias: converted from weakness to temporal prediction (promoters first, mechanoenhancers second in sustained signaling)
H3K27me3 at mechanoenhancers unverified: made explicit as FIRST falsifiable test with immediate falsification criteria
key_references: Zhang et al. 2025 (viscoelastic ECM → distinct epigenomic outcomes from elastic ECM)
Tayler et al. 2026 Mol Biol Cell (KDM6B regulation by ECM stiffness/relaxation)
Geiger et al. 2009 Nat Rev Mol Cell Biol (FAK-Src mechanotransduction)
Ntziachristos et al. 2014 Nat Med (KDM6B at distal enhancer regulatory elements)
Cosgrove et al. 2025 Science (mechanoenhancer catalog)

scores_expected_change.Novelty: +1 (viscoelasticity crossover adds new dimension not in any other hypothesis)
scores_expected_change.Testability: +0.5 (matched-modulus elastic vs viscoelastic ECM is clean comparison)
scores_expected_change.Groundedness: +0.5 (Zhang 2025 viscoelastic epigenome grounding; temporal sequence prediction is measurable)
scores_expected_change.Falsifiability: +1 (H3K27me3 premise test is now explicit first step with clear falsification criterion)

evolved_hypotheses.parent_title: Phase-Separated YAP Condensates Mediate Looping-Independent E-P Contacts
evolved_hypotheses.evolved_title: YAP Condensate Coalescence Events (Not Single-Condensate Surface Tension) Drive Multi-Locus Mechanoenhancer Co-Localization on Stiff ECM
evolved_hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: YAP-BRD4 condensate Ostwald ripening and coalescence → discrete 50-150 nm spatial jumps → progressive multi-locus mechanoenhancer co-localization without chromatin loops
evolved_hypotheses.critic_question_applied: CQ4: verteporfin replaced with IDR-deleted YAP F95A/W96A (Boija 2018) — condensate-incompetent, TEAD-binding intact
improvements_over_parent: Physics fix: single-condensate surface tension (~10 pN) insufficient to pull loci from 400→100 nm; coalescence mechanism (Ostwald ripening + merger) resolves this with discrete 50-150 nm jumps per event
CQ4 applied: verteporfin (multiple targets, photosensitizer off-targets) replaced by YAP F95A/W96A (Boija 2018) — specific condensate-incompetent, TEAD-binding intact mutant

Competing mechanism controlled: flavopiridol (CDK9 inhibitor, dissolves Pol II transcription factories) added as orthogonal perturbation to distinguish YAP condensate from transcription factory mechanism
Live imaging prediction: discrete spatial jumps in inter-locus distance during coalescence events; WT YAP: 3-5 discrete 50-150 nm jumps per cell at t=30-90 min; F95A/W96A: no discrete jumps
Coalescence timeline specified: 4-6 merger events over 30-90 min reduce inter-locus distance from ~400 nm to <100 nm in measurable steps
wounds_addressed: Verteporfin off-targets: replaced with specific IDR-deleted YAP mutant (CQ4)
Single-condensate surface tension insufficient: replaced with coalescence dynamics mechanism
Competing mechanisms (transcription factories): flavopiridol control directly tests and rules out transcription factory hypothesis
key_references: Boija et al. 2018 Cell (YAP F95A/W96A condensate-incompetent mutations; TEAD binding retained)
Brangwynne et al. 2011 PNAS (condensate coalescence dynamics in cells)
Feric et al. 2016 Cell (Ostwald ripening in nuclear condensates)
Shin et al. 2018 Cell (condensate-mediated locus co-localization; CasDrop mechanism = coalescence, not single surface tension)
Cosgrove et al. 2025 Science (86.2% loop-less mechanoenhancers; Micro-C data)
Cho et al. 2018 Cell (transcription factories as alternative mechanism)
Brahmachari 2022 Biophysical J (chromatin stiffness κ ~20 pN/nm at 100 nm scales)

scores_expected_change.Mechanistic_Specificity: +2 (coalescence dynamics specified; step size 50-150 nm; timeline 4-6 events over 30-90 min)
scores_expected_change.Testability: +2 (IDR-deleted YAP mutant + flavopiridol orthogonal control + live coalescence imaging = three independent tests)
scores_expected_change.Groundedness: +1 (Ostwald ripening grounded in Brangwynne 2011, Feric 2016; Boija 2018 mutant grounded)
scores_expected_change.Falsifiability: +1 (competing mechanism prediction + live jump test are both falsifiable)

bridge_mechanisms.EH3: BRD4 IDR multivalent LLPS cooperativity → stiffness threshold switch
bridge_mechanisms.EH4: G-actin threshold → RPEL conformational unmasking → SRF-independent EP300 recruitment
bridge_mechanisms.EH1: ECM stress relaxation rate → KDM6B nuclear retention → promoter-to-mechanoenhancer demethylation cascade
bridge_mechanisms.EH5: YAP condensate coalescence (Ostwald ripening) → discrete spatial jumps → multi-locus co-localization

diversity_check.note_EH3_EH5_overlap: Both involve YAP condensates but ask different questions: EH3=transcriptional cooperativity encoding, EH5=3D genome co-localization. Different bridge mechanisms, different predictions, different experiments. Not redundant.

scores_summary.improvement_note: All five are mechanistically stronger than parents. EH2 has the largest improvement (fatal error corrected). EH3 most likely to maintain top rank.
